quality.rationale: 基于协变导数乘积法则与联络定义的多步张量恒等式推导，适用于任意伪黎曼流形结构

origin.question:
[文件 1: 1_s7_7.7_a.md]

Problem 7.7. Prove the following identities:
(a) $\mathbf{g}_{\alpha  eta ,\gamma} = \Gamma_{\alpha} eta \gamma +\Gamma_{ eta \alpha \gamma}$

## Solution

Solution 7.7.
$$
\begin{array}{l} \mathbf {g}_{\alpha  eta , \gamma} = 
abla_{\gamma} \left(\mathbf {e}_{\alpha} \cdot \mathbf {e}_{ eta}ight) = \left(
abla_{\gamma} \mathbf {e}_{\alpha}ight) \cdot \mathbf {e}_{ eta} + \mathbf {e}_{\alpha} \cdot \left(
abla_{\gamma} \mathbf {e}_{ eta}ight) \\
= \Gamma_{\alpha \gamma}^{\mu} \mathbf {e}_{\mu} \cdot \mathbf {e}_{ eta} + \Gamma_{ eta \gamma}^{\mu} \mathbf {e}_{\mu} \cdot \mathbf {e}_{\alpha} \\
= \Gamma_{ eta a \gamma} + \Gamma_{a  eta \gamma}. \\
\end {array}
$$
$$
\begin{array}{l} \mathbf {g}_{\alpha \mu} \mathbf {g}^{\mu  eta} = \delta_{\alpha}^{ eta} \tag {b} \\
\mathbf {g}_{\alpha \mu , \gamma} \mathbf {g}^{\mu  eta} + \mathbf {g}_{\alpha \mu} \mathbf {g}^{\mu  eta},_{\gamma} = 0 \\
\mathbf {g}_{\alpha \mu} \mathbf {g}^{\mu  eta}_{, \gamma} = - \mathbf {g}_{\alpha \mu , \gamma} \mathbf {g}^{\mu  eta}. \\
\end {array}
$$
$$
\begin{array}{l} \mathrm {(c)} \quad \mathrm {g}^{\alpha  eta}_{, \gamma} = - \mathrm {g}_{\lambda \mu , \gamma} \mathrm {g}^{\mu  eta} \mathrm {g}^{\lambda \alpha} = - (\Gamma_{\lambda \mu \gamma} + \Gamma_{\mu \lambda \gamma}) \mathrm {g}^{\mu  eta} \mathrm {g}^{\lambda \alpha} \\
= - \Gamma_{\mu \gamma}^{\alpha} \mathsf {g}^{\mu  eta} - \Gamma_{\lambda \gamma}^{\beta} \mathsf {g}^{\lambda a} . \\
\end {array}
$$
(Here we have used the result in (a).)
(d) For any matrix $\| \mathbf{g}_{\alpha \beta}\|$ 
$$
\left(\log \det \left\| \mathbf {g}_{\alpha \beta} \right\|\right)_{, a} = \operatorname {T r} \left\| \mathbf {g}_{\alpha \beta} \right\|^{- 1} \left\| \mathbf {g}_{\mu 
u , a} \right\|,
$$
therefore 
$$
(\log \mathrm {g}), a = \mathrm {g}^{\mu 
u} \mathrm {g}_{\mu 
u}, a
$$
and 
$$
\frac {\mathrm {g}_{, a}}{\mathrm {g}} = \mathrm {g}^{\mu 
u} \mathrm {g}_{\mu 
u , a}.
$$
And so, by (b) 
$$
\mathrm {g}_{, a} = \mathrm {g} \mathrm {g}^{\mu 
u} \mathrm {g}_{\mu 
u , a} = - \mathrm {g} \mathrm {g}_{\mu 
u} \mathrm {g}^{\mu 
u}, a
$$
(e) In a coordinate frame, $\Gamma_{\alpha \beta}^{\mu} = \frac{1}{2}\mathbf{g}^{\mu 
u}(\mathbf{g}_{\nu \alpha ,\beta} + \mathbf{g}_{\nu \beta ,\alpha} - \mathbf{g}_{\alpha \beta ,\nu})$ and since the last two terms cancel $\Gamma_{\alpha \beta}^{\alpha} = \frac{1}{2}\mathbf{g}^{\alpha \nu}\mathbf{g}_{\nu \alpha ,\beta}$ . Thus, by (d) 
$$
\Gamma_{a \beta}^{a} = \frac {1}{2} g, \beta / g = \frac {1}{2} (\log | g |), \beta = (\log | g |^{\frac {1}{2}}), \beta .
$$
(f) $\mathbf{g}^{\mu 
u}\Gamma_{\mu 
u}^{\alpha} = -\mathbf{g}^{\alpha \beta},\beta -\Gamma^{\beta}_{\lambda \beta}\mathbf{g}^{\lambda \alpha}$ (putting $\beta = \gamma$ in (c) and summing) 
$$
\begin{array}{l} = - \mathrm {g}^{\alpha \beta}, \beta - (\log | \mathrm {g} |^{\frac {1}{2}}), \lambda \mathrm {g}^{\lambda \alpha} \quad \text {b y (e)} \\
= - \mathrm {g}_{, \nu}^{a \nu} - \left(\log \left| \mathrm {g} \right|^{\frac {1}{2}}\right)_{, \nu} \mathrm {g}^{a \nu} \quad \text {c h a n g i n g d u m m y i n d i c e s} \\
= - \mathrm {g}^{a \nu}_{, \nu} - | \mathrm {g} |^{\frac {1}{2}}_{, \nu} \mathrm {g}^{a \nu} | \mathrm {g} |^{- \frac {1}{2}} \\
= - \frac {1}{| \mathrm {g} |^{\frac {1}{2}}} \left(\mathrm {g}^{\alpha \nu} | \mathrm {g} |^{\frac {1}{2}}\right)_{, \nu}. \\
\end {array}
$$
(g) $\mathbf{A}_{;a}^{\alpha} = \mathbf{A}_{,a}^{\alpha} + \Gamma_{\beta a}^{\alpha}\mathbf{A}^{\beta} = \mathbf{A}_{,a}^{\alpha} + \frac{1}{|\mathbf{g}|^{\frac{1}{2}}}(|\mathbf{g}|^{\frac{1}{2}}),\beta \mathbf{A}^{\beta}$ by (e) 
$$
= \frac {1}{| g |^{\frac {1}{2}}} \left(| g |^{\frac {1}{2}} A^{a}\right), a
$$
$$
\begin{array}{l} \mathrm {(h)} \quad \mathrm {A}_{\alpha}^{\beta}; \beta = \mathrm {A}_{\alpha}^{\beta}, \beta + \Gamma_{\mu \beta}^{\beta} \mathrm {A}_{\alpha}^{\mu} - \Gamma_{\alpha \beta}^{\lambda} \mathrm {A}_{\lambda}^{\beta} \\
= \mathrm {A}_{\alpha}^{\beta}, \beta + \frac {1}{| \mathrm {g} |^{\frac {1}{2}}} (| \mathrm {g} |^{\frac {1}{2}}), \mu \mathrm {A}_{\alpha}^{\mu} - \Gamma_{\alpha \mu}^{\lambda} \mathrm {A}_{\lambda}^{\mu} \\
= \frac {1}{| \mathbf {g} |^{\frac {1}{2}}} \left(| \mathbf {g} |^{\frac {1}{2}} \mathbf {A}_{\alpha}^{\beta}\right), \beta - \Gamma_{\alpha \mu}^{\lambda} \mathbf {A}_{\lambda}^{\mu} \\
\end {array}
$$
$$
\begin{array}{l} \mathbf {A}^{\alpha \beta}; \beta = \mathbf {A}^{\alpha \beta}, \beta + \Gamma_{\mu \beta}^{\alpha} \mathbf {A}^{\mu \beta} + \Gamma_{\mu \beta}^{\beta} \mathbf {A}^{\alpha \mu} \tag {i} \\
= \mathrm {A}^{\alpha \beta}, \beta + \Gamma_{\mu \beta}^{\alpha} \mathrm {A}^{\mu \beta} + \frac {1}{| \mathrm {g} |^{\frac {1}{2}}} (| \mathrm {g} |^{\frac {1}{2}}), \mu \mathrm {A}^{\alpha \mu} \\
= \frac {1}{| \mathrm {g} |^{\frac {1}{2}}} \left(\mathrm {A}^{\alpha \beta} | \mathrm {g} |^{\frac {1}{2}}\right), \beta + \Gamma_{\mu \beta}^{\alpha} \mathrm {A}^{\mu \beta}. \\
\end {array}
$$
But $\Gamma_{\mu \beta}^{\alpha} = \Gamma_{(\mu \beta)}^{\alpha}$ in a coordinate frame, so if $\mathbf{A}^{\mu \beta} = \mathbf{A}^{[\mu \beta]}$ , the last term vanishes, and 
$$
\mathbf {A}^{\alpha \beta}; \beta = \frac {1}{| \mathbf {g} |^{\frac {1}{2}}} (| \mathbf {g} |^{\frac {1}{2}} \mathbf {A}^{\alpha \beta}), \beta .
$$
$$
\square S = \left(S,_{\alpha} g^{\alpha \beta}\right); \beta = \frac {1}{\left| g \right|^{\frac {1}{2}}} \left(\left| g \right|^{\frac {1}{2}} S,_{\alpha} g^{\alpha \beta}\right), \beta . \tag {j}
$$
(Here (g) has been used.)
